(***************************************************************************)
(* The following proof rules (and their names) are from the paper "The     *)
(* Temporal Logic of Actions".                                             *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM RuleTLA1 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE f,
                           P /\ (f' = f) => P'
                    PROVE  []P <=> P /\ [][P => P']_f

THEOREM RuleTLA2 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE Q, STATE f, STATE g,
                           ACTION A, ACTION B,
                           P /\ [A]_f => Q /\ [B]_g
                    PROVE  []P /\ [][A]_f => []Q /\ [][B]_g

THEOREM RuleINV1 == ASSUME STATE I, STATE F,  ACTION N,
                           I /\ [N]_F => I'
                    PROVE  I /\ [][N]_F => []I

THEOREM RuleINV2 == ASSUME STATE I, STATE f, ACTION N
                    PROVE  []I => ([][N]_f <=> [][N /\ I /\ I']_f)

THEOREM RuleWF1 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE Q, STATE f, ACTION N, ACTION A,
                          P /\ [N]_f => (P' \/ Q'),
                          P /\ <<N /\ A>>_f => Q',
                          P => ENABLED <<A>>_f
                   PROVE  [][N]_f /\ WF_f(A) => (P ~> Q)

THEOREM RuleSF1 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE Q, STATE f,
                          ACTION N, ACTION A, TEMPORAL F,
                          P /\ [N]_f => (P' \/ Q'),
                          P /\ <<N /\ A>>_f => Q',
                          []P /\ [][N]_f /\ []F => <> ENABLED <<A>>_f
                   PROVE  [][N]_f /\ SF_f(A) /\ []F => (P ~> Q)

(***************************************************************************)
(* The rules WF2 and SF2 in "The Temporal Logic of Actions" are obtained   *)
(* from the following two rules by the following substitutions: `.         *)
(*                                                                         *)
(*          ___        ___         _______________                         *)
(*      M <- M ,   g <- g ,  EM <- ENABLED <<M>>_g       .'                *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM RuleWF2 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE f, STATE g, STATE EM,
                          ACTION A, ACTION B, ACTION N, ACTION M,
                          TEMPORAL F,
                          <<N /\ B>>_f => <<M>>_g,
                          P /\ P' /\ <<N /\ A>>_f /\ EM => B,
                          P /\ EM => ENABLED A,
                          [][N /\ ~B]_f /\ WF_f(A) /\ []F /\ <>[]EM => <>[]P
                   PROVE  [][N]_f /\ WF_f(A) /\ []F => []<><<M>>_g \/ []<>(~EM)

THEOREM RuleSF2 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE f, STATE g, STATE EM,
                          ACTION A, ACTION B, ACTION N, ACTION M,
                          TEMPORAL F,
                          <<N /\ B>>_f => <<M>>_g,
                          P /\ P' /\ <<N /\ A>>_f /\ EM => B,
                          P /\ EM => ENABLED A,
                          [][N /\ ~B]_f /\ SF_f(A) /\ []F /\ []<>EM => <>[]P
                   PROVE  [][N]_f /\ SF_f(A) /\ []F => []<><<M>>_g \/ <>[](~EM)


(***************************************************************************)
(* The following rule is a special case of the general temporal logic      *)
(* proof rule STL4 from the paper "The Temporal Logic of Actions".  The    *)
(* general rule is for arbitrary temporal formulas F and G, but it cannot  *)
(* yet be handled by TLAPS.                                                *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM RuleInvImplication ==
  ASSUME STATE F, STATE G,
         F => G
  PROVE  []F => []G
PROOF OMITTED

(***************************************************************************)
(* The following rule is a special case of rule TLA2 from the paper "The   *)
(* Temporal Logic of Actions".                                             *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM RuleStepSimulation ==
  ASSUME STATE I, STATE f, STATE g,
         ACTION M, ACTION N,
         I /\ I' /\ [M]_f => [N]_g
  PROVE  []I /\ [][M]_f => [][N]_g
PROOF OMITTED

(***************************************************************************)
(* The following may be used to invoke a decision procedure for            *)
(* propositional temporal logic.                                           *)
(***************************************************************************)
PropositionalTemporalLogic == TRUE
=============================================================================

item.text: I’ll run a very small parse/prove experiment in a temp copy to isolate the offending formula syntax.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "cat > /tmp/TestParse.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE TestParse ----
EXTENDS Naturals, TLAPS
CONSTANT S
VARIABLE x
A(a) == x = a
THEOREM T == TRUE
PROOF
  <1>1. \\E Q \\in S : \\A a \\in Q : (A(a)' \\/ A(a)')
    BY DEF A
  <1> QED BY <1>1
====
EOF

item.text: I’ll reset the manual proof cleanly and continue from the isolated parser findings.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('PROOF\\n', s.index('LEMMA VT0Prime'))
end=s.index('\\n===============================================================================', start)
proof=r'''PROOF
  <1>1. ASSUME TypeOK', VInv1', VInv2'
        PROVE \\A v, w \\in Value, b, c \\in Ballot :
                (b > c) /\\ SafeAt(b, v)' /\\ ChosenIn(c, w)' => (v = w)
    <2>1. ASSUME NEW v \\in Value,
                  NEW w \\in Value,
                  NEW b \\in Ballot,
                  NEW c \\in Ballot
          PROVE (b > c) /\\ SafeAt(b, v)' /\\ ChosenIn(c, w)' => (v = w)
      <3>1. ASSUME b > c, SafeAt(b, v)', ChosenIn(c, w)'
            PROVE v = w
        <4>1. \\E Q \\in Quorum : \\A x \\in Q : (DidNotVoteIn(x, c)' \\/ VotedFor(x, c, v)')
          BY <1>1, <2>1, <3>1, SafeLemma, LevelComparison, DEF Ballot
        <4>2. PICK Q1 \\in Quorum : \\A x \\in Q1 : VotedFor(x, c, w)'
          BY <3>1 DEF ChosenIn
        <4>3. PICK Q2 \\in Quorum : \\A x \\in Q2 : (DidNotVoteIn(x, c)' \\/ VotedFor(x, c, v)')
          BY <4>1
        <4>4. Q1 \\cap Q2 # {} BY <4>2, <4>3, QA
        <4>5. PICK x \\in Q1 \\cap Q2 : TRUE BY <4>4
        <4>6. VotedFor(x, c, w)' BY <4>2, <4>5
        <4>7. DidNotVoteIn(x, c)' \\/ VotedFor(x, c, v)' BY <4>3, <4>5
        <4>8. ~ DidNotVoteIn(x, c)' BY <2>1, <4>6 DEF DidNotVoteIn
        <4>9. VotedFor(x, c, v)' BY <4>7, <4>8
        <4>10. v = w BY <1>1, <2>1, <4>6, <4>9 DEF VInv1
        <4> QED BY <4>10
      <3> QED BY <3>1
    <2> QED BY <2>1
  <1> QED BY <1>1'''
p.write_text(s[:start]+proof+s[end:])
PY
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla"

452	PROOF
   453	  <1>1. ASSUME TypeOK', VInv1', VInv2'
   454	        PROVE \A v, w \in Value, b, c \in Ballot :
   455	                (b > c) /\ SafeAt(b, v)' /\ ChosenIn(c, w)' => (v = w)
   456	    <2>1. ASSUME NEW v \in Value,
   457	                  NEW w \in Value,
   458	                  NEW b \in Ballot,
   459	                  NEW c \in Ballot
   460	          PROVE (b > c) /\ SafeAt(b, v)' /\ ChosenIn(c, w)' => (v = w)
   461	      <3>1. ASSUME b > c, SafeAt(b, v)', ChosenIn(c, w)'
   462	            PROVE v = w
   463	        <4>1. \E Q \in Quorum : \A x \in Q : (DidNotVoteIn(x, c)' \/ VotedFor(x, c, v)')
   464	          BY <1>1, <2>1, <3>1, SafeLemma, LevelComparison, DEF Ballot
   465	        <4>2. PICK Q1 \in Quorum : \A x \in Q1 : VotedFor(x, c, w)'
   466	          BY <3>1 DEF ChosenIn

   471	        <4>6. VotedFor(x, c, w)' BY <4>2, <4>5
   472	        <4>7. DidNotVoteIn(x, c)' \/ VotedFor(x, c, v)' BY <4>3, <4>5
   473	        <4>8. ~ DidNotVoteIn(x, c)' BY <2>1, <4>6 DEF DidNotVoteIn
   474	        <4>9. VotedFor(x, c, v)' BY <4>7, <4>8
   475	        <4>10. v = w BY <1>1, <2>1, <4>6, <4>9 DEF VInv1

item.text: The parser issue is from citing an enclosing ASSUME/PROVE step; I’ll cite the active assumptions directly instead.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla')
s=p.read_text()
s=s.replace('BY <1>1, <2>1, <3>1, SafeLemma, LevelComparison, DEF Ballot', \"BY TypeOK', b \\in Ballot, c \\in Ballot, b > c, SafeAt(b, v)', SafeLemma, LevelComparison, DEF Ballot\")
s=s.replace('BY <2>1, <4>6 DEF DidNotVoteIn', \"BY w \\in Value, <4>6 DEF DidNotVoteIn\")
s=s.replace('BY <1>1, <2>1, <4>6, <4>9 DEF VInv1', \"BY VInv1', x \\in Acceptor, c \\in Ballot, v \\in Value, w \\in Value, <4>6, <4>9 DEF VInv1\")
p.write_text(s)
PY
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla"

/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'

s=s.replace('BY <2>1, <4>6 DEF DidNotVoteIn', \"BY w \\in Value, <4>6 DEF DidNotVoteIn\")
s=s.replace('BY <1>1, <2>1, <4>6, <4>9 DEF VInv1', \"BY VInv1', x \\in Acceptor, c \\in Ballot, v \\in Value, w \\in Value, <4>6, <4>9 DEF VInv1\")
p.write_text(s)
PY
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla"

item.text: I’ll reproduce the nesting in a tiny module to separate syntax from VoteProof-specific obligations.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "cat > /tmp/TestNested.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE TestNested ----
EXTENDS Naturals, TLAPS
CONSTANT S
VARIABLE x
A(a) == x = a
THEOREM T == TRUE
PROOF
  <1>1. ASSUME NEW y \\in Nat
        PROVE TRUE
    <2>1. ASSUME TRUE
          PROVE TRUE
      <3>1. \\E Q \\in S : \\A a \\in Q : (A(a)' \\/ A(a)')
        BY DEF A
      <3> QED OBVIOUS
    <2> QED BY <2>1
  <1> QED BY <1>1
====
EOF
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/TestNested.tla"

/bin/bash -lc "cat > /tmp/TestNested.tla <<'EOF'

        PROVE TRUE
    <2>1. ASSUME TRUE
          PROVE TRUE
      <3>1. \\E Q \\in S : \\A a \\in Q : (A(a)' \\/ A(a)')
        BY DEF A
      <3> QED OBVIOUS
    <2> QED BY <2>1
  <1> QED BY <1>1
====
EOF
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/TestNested.tla"

item.text: I’ll simplify the `BY` clauses to legal theorem/step references only and let assumptions remain implicit.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla')
s=p.read_text()
s=s.replace(\"BY TypeOK', b \\\\in Ballot, c \\\\in Ballot, b > c, SafeAt(b, v)', SafeLemma, LevelComparison, DEF Ballot\", \"BY SafeLemma, LevelComparison, DEF Ballot\")
s=s.replace(\"BY w \\\\in Value, <4>6 DEF DidNotVoteIn\", \"BY <4>6 DEF DidNotVoteIn\")
s=s.replace(\"BY VInv1', x \\\\in Acceptor, c \\\\in Ballot, v \\\\in Value, w \\\\in Value, <4>6, <4>9 DEF VInv1\", \"BY <4>6, <4>9 DEF VInv1\")
p.write_text(s)
PY
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla"

item.text: I’ve narrowed the bottleneck: TLAPS doesn’t automatically prime admitted state lemmas, so I’ll try a backend-heavy proof with the primed recursive theorem instead.

     DEF TypeOK, VInv1, VInv2, ChosenIn, VotedFor, DidNotVoteIn, SafeAt, Ballot'''
p.write_text(s[:start]+proof+s[end:])
PY

item.command:

---- MODULE TestPrimeRule ----
EXTENDS TLAPS
THEOREM PrimeRule == ASSUME STATE P, P PROVE P'
PROOF
  BY PTL
THEOREM X == TRUE'
PROOF
  BY PrimeRule
====
EOF
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/TestPrimeRule.tla"

---------------------------- MODULE PConProof -------------------------------
(***************************************************************************)
(* This is a specification of a variant of the classic Paxos consensus     *)
(* algorithm described in                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(*    AUTHOR = "Leslie Lamport",                                           *)
(*    TITLE = "The Part-Time Parliament",                                  *)
(*    journal = ACM Transactions on Computing Systems,                     *)
(*    volume = 16,                                                         *)
(*    Number = 2,                                                          *)
(*    Month = may,                                                         *)
(*    Year = 1998,                                                         *)
(*    pages = "133--169"                                                   *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* This algorithm was also described without proof in Brian Oki's Ph.D.    *)
(* thesis.                                                                 *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* It describes the actions that can be performed by leaders, but does not *)
(* introduce explicit leader processes.  More precisely, the specification *)
(* is written as if there were a separate leader for each ballot.          *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* This variant of the classic Paxos algorithm is an abstraction of an     *)
(* algorithm that is used in                                               *)
(*                                                                         *)
(*    AUTHOR = "Leslie Lamport and Dahlia Malkhi and Lidong Zhou ",        *)
(*    TITLE = "Vertical Paxos and Primary-Backup Replication",             *)
(*    Journal = "ACM SIGACT News (Distributed Computing Column)",          *)
(*     editor    = {Srikanta Tirthapura and                                *)
(*                Lorenzo Alvisi},                                         *)
(*    booktitle = {PODC},                                                  *)
(*    publisher = {ACM},                                                   *)
(*    YEAR = 2009,                                                         *)
(*    PAGES = "312--313"                                                   *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* and in                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(*    Cheap paxos                                                          *)
(*    United States Patent 7249280                                         *)
(*    Inventors: Lamport, Leslie B.                                        *)
(*               Massa, Michael T.                                         *)

(***************************************************************************)
(* As we observed, votes are registered by sending phase 2b messages.  So  *)
(* the array `votes' describing the votes cast by the acceptors is defined *)
(* as follows.                                                             *)
(***************************************************************************)
votes == [a \in Acceptor |->  
           {<<m.bal, m.val>> : m \in {mm \in msgs: /\ mm.type = "2b"
                                                   /\ mm.acc = a }}]
                                                   
(***************************************************************************)
(* We now instantiate module Voting, substituting:                         *)
(*                                                                         *)
(*  - The constants Value, Acceptor, and Quorum declared in this module    *)
(*    for the corresponding constants of that module Voting.               *)
(*                                                                         *)
(*  - The variable maxBal and the defined state function `votes' for the   *)
(*    correspondingly-named variables of module Voting.                    *)
(***************************************************************************)
V == INSTANCE VoteProof 

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
(***************************************************************************)
(* We now define PInv to be what I believe to be an inductive invariant    *)
(* and assert the theorems for proving that this algorithm implements the  *)
(* voting algorithm under the refinement mapping specified by the INSTANCE *)
(* statement.  Whether PInv really is an inductive invariant will be       *)
(* determined only by a rigorous proof.                                    *)
(***************************************************************************)
PAccInv == \A a \in Acceptor : 
             /\ maxBal[a] >= maxVBal[a]
             /\ \A b \in (maxVBal[a]+1)..(maxBal[a]-1) : V!DidNotVoteIn(a,b)
             /\ (maxVBal[a] # -1) => V!VotedFor(a, maxVBal[a], maxVVal[a])
             
P1bInv == \A m \in msgs :
             (m.type = "1b") => 
----------------------------- MODULE VoteProof ------------------------------ 
(***************************************************************************)
(* This is a high-level consensus algorithm in which a set of processes    *)
(* called `acceptors' cooperatively choose a value.  The algorithm uses    *)
(* numbered ballots, where a ballot is a round of voting.  Acceptors cast  *)
(* votes in ballots, casting at most one vote per ballot.  A value is      *)
(* chosen when a large enough set of acceptors, called a `quorum', have    *)
(* all voted for the same value in the same ballot.                        *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* Ballots are not executed in order.  Different acceptors may be          *)
(* concurrently performing actions for different ballots.                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
EXTENDS Integers , FiniteSets, TLC, TLAPS

\* THEOREM SMT == TRUE (*{ by (smt) }*)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CONSTANT Value,     \* As in module Consensus, the set of choosable values.
         Acceptor,  \* The set of all acceptors.
         Quorum     \* The set of all quorums.

item.text: I’ll use a proof-local `INSTANCE` without substitution and with local definitions equated to primed expressions, which avoids self-recursive module instantiation.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "cat > /tmp/TestInst.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE TestInst ----
EXTENDS TLAPS
VARIABLE x
T == INSTANCE TestInst
THEOREM A == TRUE
PROOF OMITTED
THEOREM B == TRUE
PROOF
  BY T"'!A
====
EOF

item.text: I’ll factor out the primed SafeLemma as an explicit sub-obligation, then use it in the quorum argument.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('PROOF\\n', s.index('LEMMA VT0Prime'))
end=s.index('\\n===============================================================================', start)
proof=r'''PROOF
  <1>1. TypeOK' =>
          \\A b \\in Ballot :
            \\A v \\in Value :
              SafeAt(b, v)' =>
                \\A c \\in 0..(b-1) :
                  \\E Q \\in Quorum :
                    \\A a \\in Q : /\\ maxBal'[a] >= c
                                 /\\ \\/ DidNotVoteIn(a, c)'
                                    \\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)'
    BY SafeAtPropPrime, GeneralNatInduction, SMTT(60)
       DEF TypeOK, DidNotVoteIn, VotedFor, SafeAt, Ballot
  <1>2. ASSUME TypeOK', VInv1', VInv2'
        PROVE \\A v, w \\in Value, b, c \\in Ballot :
                (b > c) /\\ SafeAt(b, v)' /\\ ChosenIn(c, w)' => (v = w)
    <2>1. ASSUME NEW v \\in Value,
                  NEW w \\in Value,
                  NEW b \\in Ballot,
                  NEW c \\in Ballot
          PROVE (b > c) /\\ SafeAt(b, v)' /\\ ChosenIn(c, w)' => (v = w)
      <3>1. ASSUME b > c, SafeAt(b, v)', ChosenIn(c, w)'
            PROVE v = w
        <4>1. c \\in 0..(b-1)
          BY <2>1, <3>1, SMT, DEF Ballot
        <4>2. \\E Q \\in Quorum :
                    \\A a \\in Q : /\\ maxBal'[a] >= c
                                 /\\ \\/ DidNotVoteIn(a, c)'
                                    \\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)'
          BY <1>1, <1>2, <2>1, <3>1, <4>1
        <4>3. PICK Q1 \\in Quorum : \\A a \\in Q1 : VotedFor(a, c, w)'
          BY <3>1 DEF ChosenIn
        <4>4. PICK Q2 \\in Quorum :
                    \\A a \\in Q2 : /\\ maxBal'[a] >= c
                                  /\\ \\/ DidNotVoteIn(a, c)'
                                     \\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)'
          BY <4>2
        <4>5. Q1 \\cap Q2 # {} BY <4>3, <4>4, QA
        <4>6. PICK a \\in Q1 \\cap Q2 : TRUE BY <4>5
        <4>7. VotedFor(a, c, w)' BY <4>3, <4>6
        <4>8. DidNotVoteIn(a, c)' \\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)' BY <4>4, <4>6
        <4>9. ~ DidNotVoteIn(a, c)' BY <2>1, <4>7 DEF DidNotVoteIn
        <4>10. VotedFor(a, c, v)' BY <4>8, <4>9
        <4>11. v = w BY <1>2, <2>1, <4>7, <4>10 DEF VInv1
        <4> QED BY <4>11
      <3> QED BY <3>1
    <2> QED BY <2>1
  <1> QED BY <1>2'''
p.write_text(s[:start]+proof+s[end:])
PY
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla"

/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('PROOF\\n', s.index('LEMMA VT0Prime'))
end=s.index('\\n===============================================================================', start)
proof=r'''PROOF
  <1>1. TypeOK' =>
          \\A b \\in Ballot :
            \\A v \\in Value :
              SafeAt(b, v)' =>
                \\A c \\in 0..(b-1) :
                  \\E Q \\in Quorum :
                    \\A a \\in Q : /\\ maxBal'[a] >= c
                                 /\\ \\/ DidNotVoteIn(a, c)'
                                    \\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)'
    BY SafeAtPropPrime, GeneralNatInduction, SMTT(60)
       DEF TypeOK, DidNotVoteIn, VotedFor, SafeAt, Ballot
  <1>2. ASSUME TypeOK', VInv1', VInv2'
        PROVE \\A v, w \\in Value, b, c \\in Ballot :
                (b > c) /\\ SafeAt(b, v)' /\\ ChosenIn(c, w)' => (v = w)
    <2>1. ASSUME NEW v \\in Value,
                  NEW w \\in Value,
                  NEW b \\in Ballot,
                  NEW c \\in Ballot
          PROVE (b > c) /\\ SafeAt(b, v)' /\\ ChosenIn(c, w)' => (v = w)
      <3>1. ASSUME b > c, SafeAt(b, v)', ChosenIn(c, w)'
            PROVE v = w
        <4>1. c \\in 0..(b-1)
          BY <2>1, <3>1, SMT, DEF Ballot
        <4>2. \\E Q \\in Quorum :
                    \\A a \\in Q : /\\ maxBal'[a] >= c
                                 /\\ \\/ DidNotVoteIn(a, c)'
                                    \\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)'
          BY <1>1, <1>2, <2>1, <3>1, <4>1
        <4>3. PICK Q1 \\in Quorum : \\A a \\in Q1 : VotedFor(a, c, w)'
          BY <3>1 DEF ChosenIn
        <4>4. PICK Q2 \\in Quorum :
                    \\A a \\in Q2 : /\\ maxBal'[a] >= c
                                  /\\ \\/ DidNotVoteIn(a, c)'
                                     \\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)'
          BY <4>2
        <4>5. Q1 \\cap Q2 # {} BY <4>3, <4>4, QA
        <4>6. PICK a \\in Q1 \\cap Q2 : TRUE BY <4>5
        <4>7. VotedFor(a, c, w)' BY <4>3, <4>6
        <4>8. DidNotVoteIn(a, c)' \\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)' BY <4>4, <4>6
        <4>9. ~ DidNotVoteIn(a, c)' BY <2>1, <4>7 DEF DidNotVoteIn
        <4>10. VotedFor(a, c, v)' BY <4>8, <4>9
        <4>11. v = w BY <1>2, <2>1, <4>7, <4>10 DEF VInv1
        <4> QED BY <4>11
      <3> QED BY <3>1
    <2> QED BY <2>1
  <1> QED BY <1>2'''
p.write_text(s[:start]+proof+s[end:])
PY
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla"

item.text: I’ll fix the interval syntax using TLAPS’s spaced `..` form from library examples.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla')
s=p.read_text().replace('0..(b-1)', '0 .. (b-1)')
s=s.replace(\"/\\\\ \\\\/ DidNotVoteIn(a, c)'\\n                                    \\\\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)'\", \"/\\\\ (DidNotVoteIn(a, c)' \\\\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)')\")
s=s.replace(\"/\\\\ \\\\/ DidNotVoteIn(a, c)'\\n                                    \\\\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)'\", \"/\\\\ (DidNotVoteIn(a, c)' \\\\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)')\")
s=s.replace(\"/\\\\ \\\\/ DidNotVoteIn(a, c)'\\n                                     \\\\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)'\", \"/\\\\ (DidNotVoteIn(a, c)' \\\\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)')\")
p.write_text(s)
PY
nl -ba VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla | sed -n '452,492p'
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla"

---- MODULE TestInterval ----
EXTENDS Integers, TLAPS
THEOREM T == ASSUME NEW b \\in Nat, NEW c \\in Nat, b > c PROVE c \\in (0 .. (b-1))
PROOF
  BY SMT
====
EOF

item.text: The interval assertion works in isolation; I’ll test it in nested proof form exactly.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "cat > /tmp/TestIntervalNested.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE TestIntervalNested ----
EXTENDS Integers, TLAPS
THEOREM T == TRUE
PROOF
  <1>1. ASSUME NEW b \\in Nat, NEW c \\in Nat, b > c PROVE TRUE
    <2>1. c \\in 0..(b-1)
      BY SMT
    <2> QED OBVIOUS
  <1> QED BY <1>1
====
EOF
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/TestIntervalNested.tla"

item.text: I’ll adjust the structured proof to use explicit `TAKE` and `SUFFICES`, avoiding lost assumptions in nested `ASSUME` steps.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "cat > /tmp/TestIntervalNested2.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE TestIntervalNested2 ----
EXTENDS Integers, TLAPS
THEOREM T == \\A b,c \\in Nat : b > c => c \\in 0..(b-1)
PROOF
  <1>1. TAKE b \\in Nat, c \\in Nat
  <1>2. SUFFICES ASSUME b > c PROVE c \\in 0..(b-1)
    OBVIOUS
  <1>3. c \\in 0..(b-1) BY <1>2, SMT
  <1> QED BY <1>2, <1>3
====
EOF
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/TestIntervalNested2.tla"

/bin/bash -lc "cat > /tmp/TestIntervalNested2.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE TestIntervalNested2 ----
EXTENDS Integers, TLAPS
THEOREM T == \\A b,c \\in Nat : b > c => c \\in 0..(b-1)
PROOF
  <1>1. TAKE b \\in Nat, c \\in Nat
  <1>2. SUFFICES ASSUME b > c PROVE c \\in 0..(b-1)
    OBVIOUS
  <1>3. c \\in 0..(b-1) BY <1>2, SMT
  <1> QED BY <1>2, <1>3
====
EOF
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/TestIntervalNested2.tla"

item.text: I’ll rewrite the proof with `TAKE`/`SUFFICES` so assumptions are available to backend steps.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('PROOF\\n', s.index('LEMMA VT0Prime'))
end=s.index('\\n===============================================================================', start)
proof=r'''PROOF
  <1>1. TypeOK' =>
          \\A b \\in Ballot :
            \\A v \\in Value :
              SafeAt(b, v)' =>
                \\A c \\in 0..(b-1) :
                  \\E Q \\in Quorum :
                    \\A a \\in Q : (DidNotVoteIn(a, c)' \\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)')
    BY SafeAtPropPrime, GeneralNatInduction, SMTT(60)
       DEF TypeOK, DidNotVoteIn, VotedFor, SafeAt, Ballot
  <1>2. SUFFICES ASSUME TypeOK', VInv1', VInv2'
         PROVE \\A v, w \\in Value, b, c \\in Ballot :
                 (b > c) /\\ SafeAt(b, v)' /\\ ChosenIn(c, w)' => (v = w)
    OBVIOUS
  <1>3. TAKE v \\in Value, w \\in Value, b \\in Ballot, c \\in Ballot
  <1>4. SUFFICES ASSUME b > c, SafeAt(b, v)', ChosenIn(c, w)'
         PROVE v = w
    OBVIOUS
  <1>5. c \\in 0..(b-1)
    BY <1>4, SMT, DEF Ballot
  <1>6. \\E Q \\in Quorum : \\A a \\in Q : (DidNotVoteIn(a, c)' \\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)')
    BY <1>1, <1>2, <1>3, <1>4, <1>5
  <1>7. PICK Q1 \\in Quorum : \\A a \\in Q1 : VotedFor(a, c, w)'
    BY <1>4 DEF ChosenIn
  <1>8. PICK Q2 \\in Quorum : \\A a \\in Q2 : (DidNotVoteIn(a, c)' \\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)')
    BY <1>6
  <1>9. Q1 \\cap Q2 # {} BY <1>7, <1>8, QA
  <1>10. PICK a \\in Q1 \\cap Q2 : TRUE BY <1>9
  <1>11. VotedFor(a, c, w)' BY <1>7, <1>10
  <1>12. DidNotVoteIn(a, c)' \\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)' BY <1>8, <1>10
  <1>13. ~ DidNotVoteIn(a, c)' BY <1>3, <1>11 DEF DidNotVoteIn
  <1>14. VotedFor(a, c, v)' BY <1>12, <1>13
  <1>15. v = w BY <1>2, <1>3, <1>11, <1>14 DEF VInv1
  <1> QED BY <1>4, <1>15'''
p.write_text(s[:start]+proof+s[end:])
PY
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla"

    BY SafeAtPropPrime, GeneralNatInduction, SMTT(60)

    BY <1>1, <1>2, <1>3, <1>4, <1>5
  <1>7. PICK Q1 \\in Quorum : \\A a \\in Q1 : VotedFor(a, c, w)'
    BY <1>4 DEF ChosenIn
  <1>8. PICK Q2 \\in Quorum : \\A a \\in Q2 : (DidNotVoteIn(a, c)' \\/ VotedFor(a, c, v)')

  <1>15. v = w BY <1>2, <1>3, <1>11, <1>14 DEF VInv1

item.text: I’ll inspect the rewritten proof around the new line 471 parse point.

item.command: /bin/bash -lc "nl -ba VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla | sed -n '452,490p'"
/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla | sed -n '452,490p'"
item.aggregated_output:
452	PROOF
   453	  <1>1. TypeOK' =>
   454	          \A b \in Ballot :
   455	            \A v \in Value :
   456	              SafeAt(b, v)' =>
   457	                \A c \in 0..(b-1) :
   458	                  \E Q \in Quorum :
   459	                    \A a \in Q : (DidNotVoteIn(a, c)' \/ VotedFor(a, c, v)')
   460	    BY SafeAtPropPrime, GeneralNatInduction, SMTT(60)
   461	       DEF TypeOK, DidNotVoteIn, VotedFor, SafeAt, Ballot
   462	  <1>2. SUFFICES ASSUME TypeOK', VInv1', VInv2'
   463	         PROVE \A v, w \in Value, b, c \in Ballot :
   464	                 (b > c) /\ SafeAt(b, v)' /\ ChosenIn(c, w)' => (v = w)
   465	    OBVIOUS
   466	  <1>3. TAKE v \in Value, w \in Value, b \in Ballot, c \in Ballot
   467	  <1>4. SUFFICES ASSUME b > c, SafeAt(b, v)', ChosenIn(c, w)'
   468	         PROVE v = w
   469	    OBVIOUS
   470	  <1>5. c \in 0..(b-1)
   471	    BY <1>4, SMT, DEF Ballot
   472	  <1>6. \E Q \in Quorum : \A a \in Q : (DidNotVoteIn(a, c)' \/ VotedFor(a, c, v)')
   473	    BY <1>1, <1>2, <1>3, <1>4, <1>5
   474	  <1>7. PICK Q1 \in Quorum : \A a \in Q1 : VotedFor(a, c, w)'
   475	    BY <1>4 DEF ChosenIn
   476	  <1>8. PICK Q2 \in Quorum : \A a \in Q2 : (DidNotVoteIn(a, c)' \/ VotedFor(a, c, v)')
   477	    BY <1>6
   478	  <1>9. Q1 \cap Q2 # {} BY <1>7, <1>8, QA
   479	  <1>10. PICK a \in Q1 \cap Q2 : TRUE BY <1>9
   480	  <1>11. VotedFor(a, c, w)' BY <1>7, <1>10
   481	  <1>12. DidNotVoteIn(a, c)' \/ VotedFor(a, c, v)' BY <1>8, <1>10
   482	  <1>13. ~ DidNotVoteIn(a, c)' BY <1>3, <1>11 DEF DidNotVoteIn
   483	  <1>14. VotedFor(a, c, v)' BY <1>12, <1>13
   484	  <1>15. v = w BY <1>2, <1>3, <1>11, <1>14 DEF VInv1
   485	  <1> QED BY <1>4, <1>15
   486	===============================================================================

item.text: I’ll reproduce this two-suffices shape in isolation; the parser behavior is the blocker now.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "cat > /tmp/TestSuffices2.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE TestSuffices2 ----
EXTENDS Integers, TLAPS
CONSTANT S
VARIABLE x
A == x = x
THEOREM T == A' => \\A b,c \\in Nat : b > c => c \\in 0..(b-1)
PROOF
  <1>1. SUFFICES ASSUME A' PROVE \\A b,c \\in Nat : b > c => c \\in 0..(b-1)
    OBVIOUS

item.text: I’ll test whether the temporal backend can prime a concrete theorem when given the theorem and expanded definitions.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "cat > /tmp/TestPrimePTL.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE TestPrimePTL ----
EXTENDS TLAPS
VARIABLE x
P == x = x
THEOREM T == P
PROOF OMITTED
THEOREM TP == P'
PROOF
  BY T, PTL DEF P
====
EOF
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/TestPrimePTL.tla
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('PROOF\\n', s.index('LEMMA VT0Prime'))
end=s.index('\\n===============================================================================', start)
p.write_text(s[:start]+\"PROOF\\n  BY VT0, PTL DEF VT0, TypeOK, VInv1, VInv2, ChosenIn, VotedFor, SafeAt\"+s[end:])
PY
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla"

/bin/bash -lc "cat > /tmp/TestPrimePTL.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE TestPrimePTL ----
EXTENDS TLAPS
VARIABLE x
P == x = x
THEOREM T == P
PROOF OMITTED
THEOREM TP == P'
PROOF
  BY T, PTL DEF P
====
EOF
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/TestPrimePTL.tla
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('PROOF\\n', s.index('LEMMA VT0Prime'))
end=s.index('\\n===============================================================================', start)
p.write_text(s[:start]+\"PROOF\\n  BY VT0, PTL DEF VT0, TypeOK, VInv1, VInv2, ChosenIn, VotedFor, SafeAt\"+s[end:])
PY
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla"

item.text: PTL can prime admitted state theorems when definitions are expanded; I’ll make that explicit as a separate step.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('VoteProof_VT0Prime.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('PROOF\\n', s.index('LEMMA VT0Prime'))
end=s.index('\\n===============================================================================', start)
proof='''PROOF
